title: Mellow Smoky French Apples Tarte Tatin
description: A smoky and pillowy French dish featuring apple cider vinegar and a Mediterranean twist, perfect for a brunch mood. This tarte tatin is made with caramelized apples and a hint of smokiness from the flambe
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large skillet, melt the butter over medium heat and add the sugar, cooking until caramelized.
Add the apple cider vinegar and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Add the apples to the skillet and cook until they are tender, about 10 minutes.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the flour, eggs, and heavy cream to make the pastry dough.
Roll out the dough and place it over the apples in the skillet.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for 25 minutes.
Remove the skillet from the oven and sprinkle the smoked paprika over the top.
Use a kitchen torch to flambe the top of the tarte tatin, caramelizing the sugar.
Let the tarte tatin cool for 10 minutes before serving.
Serve the tarte tatin warm, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
